2	  "phase": "question",
3	  "questions": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "q1",
6	      "question": "Should E2E tests run against a real backend (Python server on :8080) or use Playwright route interception to mock API responses? The backend is a single-file Python HTTP server with in-memory state—no database seeding scripts exist.",
7	      "category": "design",
8	      "why_it_matters": "This is the most fundamental architectural decision. Real backend gives true integration coverage but requires starting/stopping the Python server and seeding test data. Mocked routes are faster and more deterministic but won't catch backend regressions."
9	    },
10	    {
11	      "id": "q2",
12	      "question": "If using a real backend: how should test data (markets, accounts, positions) be seeded before each test? The backend has no seed/fixture endpoint and state lives in-memory. Should we add a test-only reset/seed API, or create data via the existing POST endpoints in test setup?",
13	      "category": "design",
14	      "why_it_matters": "Without a seeding strategy, tests will be flaky or coupled to execution order. Creating data via POST endpoints is realistic but slow; a test-only endpoint is fast but adds non-production code to the server."
15	    },
16	    {
17	      "id": "q3",
18	      "question": "Should Playwright E2E tests be added to the CI pipeline? The current CI runs backend Python tests, frontend Vitest tests, and a build step. Adding E2E would require installing Playwright browsers and starting both the frontend dev server and the Python backend.",
19	      "category": "requirements",
20	      "why_it_matters": "CI integration determines whether we need a playwright.config.ts webServer config, CI-specific browser installation steps, and how much CI time budget is acceptable. Without CI, tests only run locally."
21	    },
22	    {
23	      "id": "q4",
24	      "question": "Which browsers should E2E tests target? Chromium only for speed, or also Firefox and WebKit for cross-browser coverage?",
25	      "category": "requirements",
26	      "why_it_matters": "Affects Playwright config, CI runtime (each browser adds ~30-60s of install + run time), and whether we discover browser-specific rendering/behavior issues."
27	    },
28	    {
29	      "id": "q5",
30	      "question": "The app has no authentication—session is configured by typing an Account ID and optional Agent ID into header inputs, persisted to localStorage. Should E2E tests set up sessions via localStorage injection (fast, skips UI) or by interacting with the header inputs (more realistic)?",
31	      "category": "design",
32	      "why_it_matters": "Affects test helper design and execution speed. localStorage injection is a common Playwright pattern but skips testing the session UI itself. Header input interaction tests the real flow but adds setup time to every test."
33	    },
34	    {
35	      "id": "q6",
36	      "question": "What scope of 'happy path' is expected for each view? For example, MarketDetail has ~10 distinct sections (header, probability bar, analytics charts, position card, P&L, trading panel, discussion, Bayes net graph, junction tree, event journal). Should tests verify all visible sections or just the core trading-relevant ones?",
37	      "category": "requirements",
38	      "why_it_matters": "Determines test count and granularity. Testing every section thoroughly could mean 15-20 tests for MarketDetail alone. A focused approach (header loads, trading works, key data displays) might be 4-5 tests."
39	    },
40	    {
41	      "id": "q7",
42	      "question": "Should E2E tests verify WebSocket-driven real-time updates (price subscriptions on MarketDetail, risk updates on Portfolio)? These require either a real backend pushing updates or sophisticated mock WebSocket servers.",
43	      "category": "design",
44	      "why_it_matters": "WebSocket testing is significantly more complex than HTTP request testing. If real-time updates are out of scope for 'happy path first', we can defer this and focus on initial page load state."
45	    },
46	    {
47	      "id": "q8",
48	      "question": "Where should the E2E test files live? Options: frontend/e2e/, frontend/tests/e2e/, or a top-level e2e/ directory (since tests may need to orchestrate both frontend and backend)?",
49	      "category": "design",
50	      "why_it_matters": "Affects project structure, tsconfig scope, and whether Playwright config lives in frontend/ or project root. A top-level directory makes sense if tests start the backend; frontend-scoped makes sense if tests only touch the frontend."
51	    }

80	def ws_read_frame(sock: Any) -> tuple[int, bytes] | None:
81	    """Read one WebSocket frame, unmask the payload. Returns (opcode, data) or None on EOF."""
82	    header = _recv_exact(sock, 2)
83	    if header is None:
84	        return None
85	    opcode = header[0] & 0x0F
86	    masked = bool(header[1] & 0x80)
87	    length = header[1] & 0x7F
88	    if length == 126:
89	        raw = _recv_exact(sock, 2)
90	        if raw is None:
91	            return None
92	        length = struct.unpack("!H", raw)[0]
93	    elif length == 127:
94	        raw = _recv_exact(sock, 8)
95	        if raw is None:
96	            return None
97	        length = struct.unpack("!Q", raw)[0]
98	    mask_key = b""
99	    if masked:
100	        mask_key = _recv_exact(sock, 4)
101	        if mask_key is None:
102	            return None
103	    payload = _recv_exact(sock, length)
104	    if payload is None:
105	        return None
106	    if masked:
107	        payload = bytes(b ^ mask_key[i % 4] for i, b in enumerate(payload))
108	    return opcode, payload

111	def _recv_exact(sock: Any, n: int) -> bytes | None:
112	    """Read exactly *n* bytes from *sock*, returning None on short read / error."""
113	    buf = bytearray()
114	    while len(buf) < n:
115	        try:
116	            chunk = sock.recv(n - len(buf))
117	        except OSError:
118	            return None
119	        if not chunk:
120	            return None
121	        buf.extend(chunk)
122	    return bytes(buf)

126	    """Send an unmasked text frame over *sock*."""
127	    payload = text.encode()
128	    header = bytearray()
129	    header.append(0x81)  # FIN + text opcode
130	    length = len(payload)
131	    if length < 126:
132	        header.append(length)
133	    elif length < 65536:
134	        header.append(126)
135	        header.extend(struct.pack("!H", length))
136	    else:
137	        header.append(127)

752	    """Apply the configured per-agent sliding-window rate limit."""
753	    if RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN <= 0 or not agent_id:
754	        return
755	
756	    now = time.monotonic()
757	
758	    with _RATE_LIMIT_LOCK:

760	        if bucket is None:
761	            bucket = deque()

765	        while bucket and bucket[0] <= cutoff:
766	            bucket.popleft()
767	
768	        if len(bucket) >= RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN:

5157	        extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,
5158	    ) -> None:
5159	        """Write a JSON response body with the standard headers."""
5160	        body = json.dumps(data).encode("utf-8")

5172	        write_request = resolve_write_request_agent(method, self.path, self.headers)

5179	    def _read_json_body(self) -> dict[str, Any] | None:

5194	        if not isinstance(body, dict):

5199	        """Execute one API request and translate ApiError into JSON responses."""
5200	        extra_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None

5203	            agent_id = self._enforce_write_controls(method)

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, futarchy-fleet]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: Install Python test dependencies
        run: pip install pytest

      - name: Run backend tests
        run: python3 -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short
        timeout-minutes: 5

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'

      - name: Install pnpm
        run: npm install -g pnpm

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        run: pnpm --dir frontend install

      - name: Run frontend tests
        run: pnpm --dir frontend test
        timeout-minutes: 5

      - name: Build frontend
        run: pnpm --dir frontend build
        timeout-minutes: 5
